name: Mustard and Turnip Greens
recipeInstructions:
1. Season the turnip greens and salt pork with pepper to taste.
2. Remove stems from the turnip greens and pick through them for any debris.
3. Wash the greens several times to remove any dirt.
4. Place the greens in a large pot.
5. Add 1 cup of water to the pot.
6. Add the salt pork and chopped onion.
7. Cook the greens slowly until both the meat and greens are tender.
8. Serve the greens with hot buttered cornbread. The liquid remaining in the pot, known as "Pot Likker," can also be served with the cornbread.
Note: This recipe was handed down by Delia Weathers from Gallatin, Tennessee, for the 1976 Indiana Black Bicentennial Committee's celebration of 200 years of Black Cookery in Indianapolis, IN. It was submitted by John Hartman from Indianapolis, IN on April 12, 1996.
